title: DevOps & AI Engineer
description:
We are seeking a highly autonomous, high-output DevOps & AI Engineer to design and build a pioneering AI-assisted system. This solo-developer initiative will automate the complex integration, merging, and migration of game project branches across versions, encompassing both Unreal Engine 5 and our proprietary APEX engine projects.  

You will be responsible for creating an intelligent system that not only manages Git and Perforce workflows but also integrates seamlessly with Jenkins for robust preflight validation and automated testing. Leveraging advanced LLM-powered agentic workflows, the system will classify, triage, and reliably resolve merge conflicts, moving beyond blind binary merges to intelligently interact with engine APIs and scripting environments.  

This is a critical role for someone ready to independently build a sophisticated backend agent framework and an intuitive frontend dashboard while leveraging an existing, mature CI/CD infrastructure.

 **What you’ll do*** + **Design and Build an AI-Powered Integration System:** Architect, develop, and deploy a comprehensive AI-assisted platform that automates game project branch integration across Unreal Engine 5 and APEX. The system will support both Git and Perforce version control and integrate seamlessly with Jenkins-based CI/CD workflows.

	+ **Develop Intelligent Conflict Resolution:** Implement advanced LLM-powered agentic workflows to classify, triage, and resolve merge conflicts. Focus on deep contextual understanding, multi-pass investigation, and intelligent decision-making rather than relying on blind binary merges.

	+ **Engine and Scripting Automation:** Leverage native Python scripting and engine APIs to interact directly with Unreal Engine, automate tasks such as Blueprint recompilation and contract-change updates, and extend these capabilities to proprietary engines such as APEX.

	+ **Full-Stack Development and Workflow Orchestration:** Independently build both the backend agent framework and an internal, project-specific frontend dashboard. Design and manage complex state machines for "Paused Integration" workflows, enabling human-in-the-loop intervention and continuous learning through self-reflection mechanisms based on human resolutions.

	+ **Define and Track Success Metrics:** Establish, monitor, and refine key performance indicators (KPIs) that measure AI effectiveness, including Attempt Rate, Escalation Rate, and Rollback Rate, ensuring continuous improvement, operational efficiency, and system reliability.
  

**Who you are:*** + **Version Control & CI/CD Mastery:** Deep expertise in both Perforce and Git, with a strong understanding of their distinct workflows and underlying models, as well as hands-on experience designing and authoring Jenkins pipelines for robust CI/CD processes within mature development infrastructures.

	+ **Game Engine & Asset Pipeline Knowledge:** Working knowledge of Unreal Engine 5 project structures and asset pipelines, including a strong understanding of the challenges associated with merging game-specific assets such as .uasset files, Blueprints, and other engine-generated content.

	+ **Applied AI/ML Practitioner:** Proven ability to build practical applications using LLMs through APIs, including prompt engineering, context engineering, agentic frameworks, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). Experience applying these techniques to areas such as code-diff analysis, semantic understanding, and automated conflict resolution, with a focus on orchestration rather than model training.

	+ **Polyglot Developer & Systems Integrator:** Proficient in Python, C# for engine tooling, and C++, with full-stack web development experience (e.g., React and Node.js) for internal dashboard development. Skilled at orchestrating complex systems and integrations across multiple platforms and APIs, including version control systems, CI/CD tools, Jira, and Slack.

	+ **Autonomous & Strategic Thinker:** A highly self-directed professional capable of independently driving complex projects, strategically leveraging AI orchestration to delegate work to specialized agent sub-systems, and designing resilient architectures with robust state management.

	+ **Game Industry Experience:** Demonstrated experience in the game industry or engine development, with a strong appreciation for the unique technical and workflow complexities of game production.
*Note: Research shows under-represented groups in the industry might not apply to a job if they don’t check off every requirement. At Avalanche, we’re more about your drive to learn and interest in tackling challenges than what’s on your resume! So don’t stress if you don’t tick every box— shoot us your application anyway! Diverse opinions and different experiences are what make our teams so great, not itemized lists of qualifications.*

**The Stockholm location**
Our Stockholm office is situated in Södermalm, the beating heart of the city’s game industry. It’s a physical representation of our games – the lush vegetation of Just Cause and theHunter: Call of the Wild meets corroded metal straight out of Mad Max. Our hybrid model requires you to be in the office on Mondays, Tuesdays, and Thursdays to foster collaboration and creativity.

**Our values**
At Avalanche Studios Group, we create worlds beyond limits. But that’s not exclusive to our games, so we’re committed to developing a diverse and inclusive workplace. All Avalanchers have a shared responsibility to create an open work environment where everyone is treated equally and respectfully. Being part of our world is not contingent on your heritage, your gender, or sexual orientation. It’s all about your passion and creativity.

**How to apply**
To apply for this position, provide your CV in English. We review applications continuously. All further studio-related information is provided under a non-disclosure agreement.

*At Avalanche Studios Group we're always on the lookout for talented individuals to join our passionate team, but please note that relocation assistance is not available for this role.*
company_description: Avalanche Studios is the original creator of the award-winning Just Cause franchise (published by Square Enix) with the third entry release

title: Saab söker erfarna ingenjörer inom mjukvara!
description:
*Drivs du av att se dina idéer bli verklighet? Välkommen till vår spännande och teknikintensiva värld.*

Din roll


För oss är teknik mer än kod och mekanik. Det är hjärtat i vår verksamhet. Det som utmanar oss, inspirerar oss och håller oss engagerade. Du kommer att vara involverad under hela produktens livscykel, från design och kodning till testning och implementering – samtidigt som du tillsammans med ditt team är en viktig pusselbit i vår resa mot att skapa nästa generations försvarssystem.


Vi erbjuder en stimulerande arbetsmiljö där du kommer att få möjlighet att utvecklas och växa som utvecklare. Du kommer att vara del av ett agilt team som stöttar dig och din personliga utveckling. Denna roll innebär samarbete, inte bara med dina närmaste kollegor, utan även till andra avdelningar, leverantörer och kunder.


Din profil


För att trivas på Saab tror du på en kultur av nyfikenhet, tillit och lagarbete. Det spelar ingen roll om du är i början av din karriär eller är expert inom ditt område, det viktigaste är att du delar vår passion för utveckling och ny spännande teknik!


Som en del av vårt team kommer du att få möjlighet att arbeta med de senaste verktygen och teknologierna inom branschen. Vi söker dig med kunskap och erfarenhet inom bland annat programmeringsspråk och verktyg såsom Python, C, C++, C# och .NET. Erfarenhet av hårdvarunära programmering, AI och maskininlärning är meriterande.


Vi arbetar även med moderna utvecklingsverktyg och tekniska plattformar som Git, DevOps och Jira. Våra team är mångsidiga, och vi tror på att olika perspektiv och erfarenheter bidrar till att skapa ännu bättre lösningar. Oavsett din tidigare arbetsbakgrund är du varmt välkommen att vara med och forma framtidens teknologi!


På vårt kontor i Karlskoga erbjuder vi en bred variation av spännande roller inom mjukvara. Oavsett om du är *mjukvaruutvecklare, mjukvaruingenjör, testledare, applikationsingenjör, cybersäkerhetsingenjör eller testingenjör*, finns det möjligheter för dig att växa och utvecklas tillsammans med oss!


Befattningen kräver att du genomgår och godkänns enligt vid var tid gällande bestämmelser för säkerhetsskydd. För befattningar där Saab har krav på säkerhetsklassinplacering kan, i förekommande fall, medföra krav på visst medborgarskap.


Det du blir en del av


Bakom våra innovationer finns alla de som har gjort det möjligt. Modiga pionjärer och kluriga tänkare. Vardagshjältar och kreativa problemlösare. De som delar djup kunskap och de som utforskar nya områden. Och alla däremellan.


Här har du chansen att påverka och bidra på ditt unika sätt. Allt från att utveckla kod och bygga imponerande försvars- och säkerhetslösningar till att ta en kaffe med en kollega. Varje bidrag räknas. Vi ger dig möjlighet att anta utmaningar och skapa smarta innovationer. I vår vänliga och tekniksmarta värld finns plats för dig att växa. Vi finns här för att tillsammans skapa trygghet för människor och samhällen.


Saab är ett ledande försvars- och säkerhetsföretag med uppdrag att hjälpa nationer att skydda sin befolkning och bidrar till trygghet för människor och samhällen. Med 28 000 talangfulla medarbetare utvecklar Saab teknik och lösningar för en säkrare och mer hållbar värld.


Saab utvecklar, tillverkar och underhåller avancerade system inom flygteknik, vapen, ledningssystem, sensorer och undervattenssystem. Saab har sitt huvudkontor i Sverige men en global verksamhet där Saab är en del av många nationers försvarsförmåga. Läs mer om oss här.


Vad innebär en spontanansökan?


Detta är en öppen ansökan för dig som är nyfiken på vår verksamhet i Karlskoga. Om du vill bli en del av vår framtida resa är du varmt välkommen att skicka in en spontanansökan. Genom att göra det visar du att du är öppen för jobbmöjligheter hos oss – och om vi längre fram får in en roll som stämmer överens med din bakgrund och dina intressen, kan vi komma att kontakta dig.  

  

Vi vill dock tydliggöra att en spontanansökan inte är detsamma som att söka en tjänst. För att maximera dina möjligheter uppmanar vi dig att visa oss vad som lockar dig mest, genom att aktivt söka de specifika tjänster som du är intresserad av!


Du kan alltid dra tillbaka din spontanansökan genom att logga in på din Workday-profil. Läs mer om dina rättigheter och hur du kontaktar Saabs dataskyddsombud här.
company_description: Saab serves the global market with world-leading products, services and solutions from military defense to civil security.

title: Senior Manual QA Engineer
description:
BrainRocket is a global company creating end-to-end tech products for clients across Fintech, iGaming, and Marketing. ‍Young, ambitious, and unstoppable, we've already taken Cyprus, Malta, Portugal, Poland, and Serbia by storm. Our BRO team consists of 1,300 bright minds creating innovative ideas and products. We don’t follow formats. We shape them. We build what works, launch it fast, and make sure it hits.

**✅ Duties and opportunities:**


* Perform end-to-end manual testing of the core platform features, APIs, and integrations.
* Collaborate with the team to evolve and implement changes to the overall platform test strategy.
* Investigate and localize issues, working closely with devtools, logs and databases.
* Manage defects in the bug-tracking system and oversee them through the entire lifecycle.
* Support release process.
* Actively collaborate with cross-functional teams (Development, DevOps, Product) to identify risks and improve product quality at early stages.
* Mentor junior/middle QA engineers and contribute to QA process improvements within the platform team.


✅ **Requirements**:


* 4+ years of experience in software testing.
* Mandatory experience in backend testing and a deep understanding of client-server architecture.
* Strong experience in Web and Mobile testing
* Solid SQL knowledge (ability to write complex queries, joins, and work with databases directly).
* Experience in defining the scope of testing, maintaining test documentation (Test Plans, Test Cases, Checklists).
* Experience in contributing to and evolving existing test strategies, as well as implementing process improvements.
* Advanced analytical troubleshooting skills, attention to detail, and a scientific approach to problem-solving (e.g., reading logs, analyzing network traffic).
* Proficiency with JIRA and Confluence.
* Strong verbal and written communication skills to effectively collaborate with developers, architects, and product managers.
* Fluent level of English (both verbal and written).
* Native level of Swedish (both verbal and written).


**✅ Will be a plus:**


* Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and tools.
* Experience with Allure TestOps as TMS.


✅ **We offer excellent benefits, including but not limited to:**


* Learning and development opportunities and interesting challenging tasks
* 30 non-service delivery days per year
* Language classes (partially paid by the Company)
* Birthday, wedding, and newborn gifts
* Career growth opportunities

Bold moves start here. Make yours. Apply today!


**By submitting your application, you agree to our** **Privacy Policy.**

title: ML Engineer
description:
We now have a new requirement and are looking for a consultant colleague with the qualifications listed below. The position is part of our consulting business, which means you will be employed by us and work either with clients or on internal projects and assignments.

*As part of your application, you may be presented to clients/partners as part of the recruitment process. If selected for the assignment, you will be employed by us and work either with clients or on internal projects and assignments as part of our consulting business.*

 **Your Profile (Must-have requirements)**  

* Strong programming skills, with extensive experience in Python
* Proven experience in machine learning and data science (e.g., classification, clustering, feature engineering, anomaly detection, neural networks)
* Experience working with LLMs, including prompting and agent-based workflows
* Solid understanding of classical ML algorithms (e.g., SVM, Random Forest, Naive Bayes, k-NN)
* Experience with ML frameworks and libraries such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, Keras, Scikit-learn, NumPy, and Pandas
* Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills
* Ability to balance creativity with practical delivery in existing systems
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills
* Proactive, ownership-driven mindset with the ability to adapt and deliver in a fast-paced environment

  

**Meritorious**  

* Experience with Scala or Java
* Knowledge in one or more of the following areas: NLP, information retrieval, data mining
* Experience in statistical modeling and building predictive models

  

**Application deadline:** 30-06-2026 **About Us**  

We currently employ experienced consultants in areas such as System Development, AI, Machine Learning, Testing, Cloud, Infra DevOps, and IT Project Management.  

  

Working at Deploja is something entirely different from working at an ordinary consulting company—and we are really proud of that. So, what makes us unique?  

  

We simply offer more than just a high salary and greater freedom; that’s just the beginning. With us, you’ll find the work environment and opportunities you deserve. Welcome to Deploja, where the grass is indeed greener!

 **Vi erbjuder dig följande, Valet är ditt!**


Exciting assignments with our many clients. We focus on long-term projects, which gives you the opportunity to create value for the client while also developing yourself.


A secure monthly salary – together we’ll agree on a base salary depending on your assignments.


Company car – we encourage you to choose an electric car for a more sustainable future.


Occupational pension and great opportunities for salary conversion.


Vacation – Would you like more than 30 days of vacation, perhaps 60 days?


Private health insurance, accident insurance, and life insurance for increased security.


Skills development – you set your own budget and choose the training you wish to attend.


Quality of life – Are you struggling to balance everyday life and wish to work a little less while still earning as much, if not more?

*We handle applications continuously, and due to the high workload, it's especially important to include a short motivation for the position, explaining how you meet the requirements.*

title: Senior Fullstack Engineer with Frontend Focus
description:
**Join our highly skilled team of developers - breaking new ground and shaping the future of retail.**  

  

**We dare to dream big, we have the courage to keep raising the bar, and we’re committed to being a force for good in retail - helping both established and fast-growing brands meet the ever-changing expectations of their customers.**  

  

**Sitoo is a fast-growing technology company exploring how far we can take innovation to deliver the world’s best Unified Commerce Platform and Point of Sale. Our technology is used in more than 20 countries - a number that keeps growing thanks to our incredible team of engineers. And we have no intention of slowing down. Are you the Software Engineer we’re looking for?**

### **Your Mission at Sitoo**


As a Fullstack Engineer at Sitoo, you'll play a vital role in building our next-generation web products. You'll join a small, agile team focused on developing the world's best Point of Sales and Unified Commerce Platform. Your work will span front and backend applications built with React, TypeScript, and Node.js. In our small team environment, your impact and influence will be significant.

### **What you'll do**

* Own, develop and operate Sitoo’s web products
* Collaborate with the team to innovate and improve processes and tooling
* Build a scalable infrastructure in AWS
* Write tests to ensure product quality
* Manage CI/CD pipelines on GitHub


Participate in development guilds

### **What you'll bring to the table**

* 5+ years of experience as a professional fullstack developer building and maintaining web applications
* Experience writing Node.js services and frontend apps with TypeScript
* A background working in fast-growing or scaling environments
* Experience in cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP)
* Fluency in English, both written and spoken

### **Bonus**

* Azure DevOps, Github Actions or GitLab CI/CD
* IaC with CDK or similar
* E2E tests with Playwright
* AI-assisted integrated development environment or agentic coding tools like Claude Code, GitHub Copilot or Cursor
* Working with design systems / documentation in Storybook

### **Why Sitoo**


We are forward thinkers. By turning curiosity into knowledge — and knowledge into continuous improvement — we break new ground and shape the future of retail. We believe in an inclusive culture where everyone feels like they belong. We support each other, win together, celebrate success together, and overcome challenges together.  

  

Today, we are close to 100 dedicated experts with a positive and solution-oriented mindset. Most importantly, we take care of each other, and we never forget to have fun.  

  

*This is a hybrid role based in Stockholm or Gothenburg. Please note that we are currently unable to offer relocation support for this position, so you’ll need to hold a permanent residence permit in Sweden.*

title: Service Reliability Engineer
description:
For our client WirelessCar, we are looking for a Service Reliability Engineer with experience in operational coordination, incident management, and process improvement.


You will be part of the Service Reliability Engineering team (“Team Ninja”), working to ensure stable and reliable connected vehicle services for global automotive customers.


The role combines reactive operational support with proactive improvement work. You will coordinate major incidents together with DevOps teams, ensure clear communication toward stakeholders and customers, facilitate post-incident reviews, and drive improvements within operational processes and reliability practices.


A key part of the role is helping DevOps teams improve operational readiness and incident handling capabilities over time. The team also works continuously with improving operational workflows, reliability processes, and ways of working across the organization.


We are looking for someone who is structured, communicative, and comfortable working in dynamic environments where collaboration and coordination are essential. You enjoy driving progress, creating clarity during operational events, and working closely with both technical teams and stakeholders.

**We believe you have:**

* Experience working with business-critical 24x7 services
* Experience with Incident Management and Problem Management processes
* Strong communication and stakeholder management skills
* Experience coordinating operational activities across multiple teams
* Experience driving operational or process improvements
* Fluent English, spoken and written

**Meritorious**

* Experience from SRE, Service Operations, DevOps, or similar operational roles
* Experience facilitating Root Cause Analysis or post-incident reviews
* Familiarity with observability or operational support platforms
* Experience working in Agile organizations
* Understanding of modern cloud-based service environments


You will work closely with other SRE and DevOps teams in a collaborative environment focused on operational excellence and continuous improvement.

title: DevOps Engineer
description:
*Want to influence the bigger picture? At Saab, you can realise amazing innovations that keep people and societies safe. Together, we can truly make an impact – and your part matters.*
**Your role**
 At Saab, we operate in an increasingly complex and rapidly evolving security environment, where technological superiority, resilience, and operational readiness are critical to safeguarding society and national interests. As Saab continues its international growth journey, digital capabilities are becoming fundamental to enabling mission-critical operations, accelerating innovation, and strengthening strategic advantage across all domains.
   

  

 Technology is no longer a support function — it is a core operational capability. The ability to securely collect, process, distribute, and operationalize data at scale is essential across Saab’s product portfolio and future defense capabilities. By integrating cloud, automation, cyber, and software-defined platforms deeper into our operational ecosystem, we enable faster decision-making, increased operational agility, and resilient mission execution.
   

  

 Key focus areas within Saab Technology include:
   

  

* Accelerating transformation across products, platforms, and engineering workflows
* Strengthening capabilities within cloud, cyber, automation, AI, edge computing, and space technologies
* Enabling secure, scalable, and mission-ready software delivery
* Building a high-performance culture centered around collaboration, accountability, and continuous improvement


 As a DevSecOps Engineer, you will play a central role in enabling secure and scalable mission-critical platforms. You will automate and optimize development and operational environments, design resilient container-based infrastructures, and contribute to ensuring that Saab’s digital capabilities remain robust, secure, and operationally available under demanding conditions.
   

  

 You will work closely with developers, platform teams, security specialists, and operational stakeholders to enable modern software delivery pipelines and cloud-native capabilities that support Saab’s future operational systems.
   

  

**Your main responsibilities include:**
* Explore, designing and operating all from secure commercial cloud technologies to air gapped technologies with a primary focus on secure public, private, and hybrid cloud platforms out to the edge.
* Building and maintaining automated CI/CD pipelines to enable rapid, reliable, and secure software delivery
* Collaborating across DevSecOps and platform engineering teams to proactively identify, analyze, and resolve system vulnerabilities and operational issues
* Monitoring, optimizing, and hardening system performance to ensure scalability, resilience, and high availability
* Developing secure and reproducible infrastructure using Infrastructure as Code (IaC) technologies such as Terraform and Ansible
* Supporting the implementation of modern cloud-native and edge-based operational environments
* Contributing to operational readiness through documentation, automation, knowledge sharing, and continuous improvement
* Advising and supporting internal and external stakeholders on secure deployment and operation of containerized workloads and runtimes


 You will be part of the Cloud Enablement & Runtime department within Common Tech - a specialized team driving strategic technology capabilities, platform enablement, and modern engineering practices supporting Saab’s long-term operational and digital transformation objectives.
   

  

**Your profile**
 You are an experienced Platform Engineer and/or DevSecOps professional with a strong interest in modern mission-critical technologies and operationally resilient systems. You thrive in environments where security, reliability, and collaboration are essential, and you are motivated by contributing to solutions that make a real-world impact.
   

  

 You take ownership, act proactively, and contribute to building a culture of trust, technical excellence, and continuous learning. You understand the importance of balancing speed, security, and operational stability in complex environments.
   

  

**Required skills and experience:**
* Kubernetes and modern container orchestration technologies
* Strong experience with automation, CI/CD pipelines, and DevSecOps practices
* Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using technologies such as Terraform or Ansible
* Experience working with public cloud and hybrid cloud platforms
* Monitoring, observability, and logging solutions such as Prometheus and Grafana
* Strong analytical, troubleshooting, and problem-solving capabilities
* Fluent in Swedish and English, both written and spoken


**Additional valuable experience:**
* Agile software engineering methodologies
* Infrastructure and platform operations including networking, storage, and virtualization
* Experience from security-sensitive, regulated, or mission-critical environments
* Understanding of cloud-native security principles and Zero Trust architectures
* Experience with edge computing, resilient systems, or disconnected environments


*This position requires that you pass a security vetting based on the current regulations around/of security protection. For positions requiring security clearance additional obligations on citizenship may apply.*
**What You Will Be Part Of**
 Explore a wealth of possibilities. Take on challenges, create smart inventions, and grow beyond. This is a place for curious minds, brave pioneers, and everyone in between. Together, we achieve the extraordinary, each bringing our unique perspectives. Your part matters.
   

  

 Saab is a leading defense and security company with an enduring purpose, to help nations keep their people and society safe. Empowered by its 28,000 talented people, Saab constantly pushes the boundaries of technology to create a safer and more sustainable world.
   

  

 Saab designs, manufactures and maintains advanced systems in aeronautics, weapons, command and control, sensors and underwater systems. Saab is headquartered in Sweden. It has major operations all over the world and is part of the domestic defense capability of several nations. Read more about us here

title: Founding Engineer
description:
We’re looking for a Founding Engineer to help build Mímr Analytics.
 



  





 Mímr is building graph-native entity intelligence for understanding companies, ownership, people and risk across fragmented data sources.
 



  





 We have early customer pull, access to relevant data sources, and are now preparing for design partner and pilot work.
 



  





 Our first wedge is audit, advisory and compliance workflows, where teams need better ways to understand complex ownership structures, cross-border relationships, source confidence and risk signals.
 



  





 We are now moving from discovery and prototype work toward pilot-ready product development. That means the technical work becomes much more serious.
 



  





 We are looking for someone who wants to help build the core platform:
 


* data ingestion from external APIs and registries
* backend services and product architecture
* entity resolution and data quality
* graph modelling and relationship intelligence
* scalable workflows for analysts and risk teams
* AI-native development, where useful, but with strong engineering fundamentals



  





 This is an early-stage role. We are not looking for someone who only wants a clearly defined job description. We are looking for someone who wants to own hard technical problems, make pragmatic decisions, and help shape the company from the ground up.
 



  





 Relevant backgrounds could include backend engineering, data engineering, graph databases, ML/data infrastructure, distributed systems, or technical product building in B2B SaaS.
 



  





 We are based in Stockholm. Early involvement would likely start part-time or project-based. At this stage, this is not yet a salaried role, but meaningful equity/options can be discussed for the right long-term person as the company develops.
 



  





 If this sounds interesting, send us your application. Especially if you have built real systems, enjoy complex data problems, and want to work on something with clear societal relevance.

title: DevOps & AI Engineer
description:
We are seeking a highly autonomous, high-output DevOps & AI Engineer to design and build a pioneering AI-assisted system. This solo-developer initiative will automate the complex integration, merging, and migration of game project branches across versions, encompassing both Unreal Engine 5 and our proprietary APEX engine projects.
   

  

 You will be responsible for creating an intelligent system that not only manages Git and Perforce workflows but also integrates seamlessly with Jenkins for robust preflight validation and automated testing. Leveraging advanced LLM-powered agentic workflows, the system will classify, triage, and reliably resolve merge conflicts, moving beyond blind binary merges to intelligently interact with engine APIs and scripting environments.
   

  

 This is a critical role for someone ready to independently build a sophisticated backend agent framework and an intuitive frontend dashboard while leveraging an existing, mature CI/CD infrastructure.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
* Design and Build an AI-Powered Integration System: Architect, develop, and deploy a comprehensive AI-assisted platform that automates game project branch integration across Unreal Engine 5 and APEX. The system will support both Git and Perforce version control and integrate seamlessly with Jenkins-based CI/CD workflows.
* Develop Intelligent Conflict Resolution: Implement advanced LLM-powered agentic workflows to classify, triage, and resolve merge conflicts. Focus on deep contextual understanding, multi-pass investigation, and intelligent decision-making rather than relying on blind binary merges.
* Engine and Scripting Automation: Leverage native Python scripting and engine APIs to interact directly with Unreal Engine, automate tasks such as Blueprint recompilation and contract-change updates, and extend these capabilities to proprietary engines such as APEX.
* Full-Stack Development and Workflow Orchestration: Independently build both the backend agent framework and an internal, project-specific frontend dashboard. Design and manage complex state machines for "Paused Integration" workflows, enabling human-in-the-loop intervention and continuous learning through self-reflection mechanisms based on human resolutions.
* Define and Track Success Metrics: Establish, monitor, and refine key performance indicators (KPIs) that measure AI effectiveness, including Attempt Rate, Escalation Rate, and Rollback Rate, ensuring continuous improvement, operational efficiency, and system reliability.


**Who You Are**
* Version Control & CI/CD Mastery: Deep expertise in both Perforce and Git, with a strong understanding of their distinct workflows and underlying models, as well as hands-on experience designing and authoring Jenkins pipelines for robust CI/CD processes within mature development infrastructures.
* Game Engine & Asset Pipeline Knowledge: Working knowledge of Unreal Engine 5 project structures and asset pipelines, including a strong understanding of the challenges associated with merging game-specific assets such as .uasset files, Blueprints, and other engine-generated content.
* Applied AI/ML Practitioner: Proven ability to build practical applications using LLMs through APIs, including prompt engineering, context engineering, agentic frameworks, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). Experience applying these techniques to areas such as code-diff analysis, semantic understanding, and automated conflict resolution, with a focus on orchestration rather than model training.
* Polyglot Developer & Systems Integrator: Proficient in Python, C# for engine tooling, and C++, with full-stack web development experience (e.g., React and Node.js) for internal dashboard development. Skilled at orchestrating complex systems and integrations across multiple platforms and APIs, including version control systems, CI/CD tools, Jira, and Slack.
* Autonomous & Strategic Thinker: A highly self-directed professional capable of independently driving complex projects, strategically leveraging AI orchestration to delegate work to specialized agent sub-systems, and designing resilient architectures with robust state management.
* Game Industry Experience: Demonstrated experience in the game industry or engine development, with a strong appreciation for the unique technical and workflow complexities of game production.


 Note: Research shows under-represented groups in the industry might not apply to a job if they don’t check off every requirement. At Avalanche, we’re more about your drive to learn and interest in tackling challenges than what’s on your resume! So don’t stress if you don’t tick every box— shoot us your application anyway! Diverse opinions and different experiences are what make our teams so great, not itemized lists of qualifications.
   

  

**The Stockholm location**
 Our Stockholm office is situated in Södermalm, the beating heart of the city’s game industry. It’s a physical representation of our games – the lush vegetation of Just Cause and theHunter: Call of the Wild meets corroded metal straight out of Mad Max. Our hybrid model requires you to be in the office on Mondays, Tuesdays, and Thursdays to foster collaboration and creativity.
   

  

**Our values**
 At Avalanche Studios Group, we create worlds beyond limits. But that’s not exclusive to our games, so we’re committed to developing a diverse and inclusive workplace. All Avalanchers have a shared responsibility to create an open work environment where everyone is treated equally and respectfully. Being part of our world is not contingent on your heritage, your gender, or sexual orientation. It’s all about your passion and creativity.
   

  

**How To Apply**
 To apply for this position, please register below and provide your CV in English. We review applications continuously. All further studio-related information is provided under a non-disclosure agreement.
   

  

 At Avalanche Studios Group we're always on the lookout for talented individuals to join our passionate team, but please note that relocation assistance is not available for this role.

title: Platform Engineer Kubernetes
description:
*Vill du vara med och vidareutveckla en robust, säker och skalbar containerplattform i framkant? Hos oss får du arbeta med modern teknik och vara en nyckelspelare i vår satsning på Kubernetes och OpenShift. Om du trivs i gränslandet mellan infrastruktur, utveckling, säkerhet och cloud native-teknik – då är detta rollen för dig!*
**Om jobbet**
 Som Platform Engineer till vårt team på Länsförsäkringar AB i Stockholm får du en central roll i vår växande platform engineering-funktion. Du arbetar med drift, utveckling och automation av vår containerplattform byggd på OpenShift och säkerställer en stabil och effektiv plattform för utvecklingsteam och applikationsdrift. Du blir en del av ett kompetent och positivt team som driver innovation och utveckling inom containerplattformar, där du får arbeta med moderna verktyg och har goda möjligheter att utvecklas.
   

  

 Du kommer ingå i ett team med med produktägare, arkitekter och andra platform engineers där du rapporterar till chefen för Public Cloud Platforms. Ni har fokus på OpenShift/Azure RedHat OpenShift, Azure och AWS. Enheten Digital & IT inom Länsförsäkringar AB levererar förstklassiga IT-lösningar och digitala tjänster till de lokala Länsförsäkringsbolagen, vilket innebär breda kontaktytor inom hela LF-federationen.
   

  

 Dina arbetsuppgifter kommer vara bland annat:
   

  

* Underhålla och vidareutveckla vår Kubernetes/OpenShift-baserade containerplattform.
* Arbeta med CI/CD-pipelines och GitOps för att förenkla och automatisera deployment och drift.
* Samarbeta med utvecklare och arkitekter för att optimera plattformens prestanda och säkerhet.
* Stötta med expertis i incident- och felsökningsarbete.
* Arbeta med onboarding och utbildning av våra DevOps-team för att säkerställa att de effektivt kan använda plattformen.


 Följande kommer göra dig framgångsrik i denna tjänst:
   

  

* Erfaren hands-on Kubernetes-expert med minst 3 års erfarenhet av Kubernetes och containerorkestrering, gärna inom finanssektorn.
* Tidigare erfarenhet av att arbeta i gränslandet mellan infrastruktur, utveckling, säkerhet och cloud native-teknik.
* Kunskap om CI/CD-pipelines, GitOps och automation.
* Erfarenhet av att arbeta i Microsoft Azure.
* Grundläggande förståelse för nätverk, lagring och säkerhet i Kubernetes-miljöer.
* Goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska, både tal och skrift.


 Meriterande är även om du har:
   

  

* Du har kompetens på AWS plattformar.


 Som person är du positiv, initiativtagande och självgående, samtidigt som du är en god team-player. Du är lösningsorienterad, ansvarstagande och nyfiken, med en stark vilja att automatisera och förbättra arbetsflöden. Du trivs med att arbeta både självständigt och i team, och har god kommunikativ och samarbetsinriktad förmåga.
   

  

**Kontaktuppgifter:**
 Rekryterande chef: Klaus Seidel, klaus.seidel@lansforsakringar.se
   

  

 Facklig representant Forena: Jessica Lööw, jessica.loow@lansforsakringar.se
   

  

 Facklig representant Akademikerföreningen: Lisa-Maria Carensjö, lisa-maria.carensjo@lansforsakringar.se
   

  

*Låter det spännande? Då vill vi veta mer om dig! Välkommen med din ansökan redan idag. Vi arbetar löpande med denna rekrytering och kan komma att tillsätta tjänsten innan sista ansökningsdag.*
 Länsförsäkringar AB är en inkluderande arbetsplats där mod, möjligheter och engagemang står i centrum. Vi erbjuder ett hållbart arbetsliv med flexibla villkor och satsar på att alla medarbetare ska kunna växa och trivas, oavsett bakgrund.
   

  

 Digital & IT är hjärtat i Länsförsäkringsgruppens digitala utveckling. Vi driver den gemensamma digitala transformationen och levererar förstklassiga IT-lösningar, datadrivna insikter och innovativa digitala tjänster som skapar värde för hela verksamheten. Vårt uppdrag är att bygga en robust och framtidssäker IT-miljö – både på plats och i molnet – som möjliggör kundnära och effektiva affärer i hela gruppen.
   

  

 Hos oss möts teknik, affär och innovation. Vi arbetar tvärfunktionellt ute i verksamheten i agila team där utveckling, drift och strategi går hand i hand. Vi ansvarar för allt från IT-styrning, enterprisearkitektur och leverantörshantering till applikationsutveckling, cybersäkerhet och verksamhetsberedskap.

title: Lead Technical Engineer – Event Integrations

 Shaping the future of fashion with people, data, and tech. The fashion and retail industries are going through a transformation, driven by customers' technology and sustainability expectations. Being a major player gives us countless opportunities to make a real impact and shape the future.

title: Platform Engineer - Cloud, Automation & AI
description:
Tobii is the global leader in eye tracking and a pioneer in attention computing. For more than 20 years, we have built technology that helps understand human attention and intent. Our technology is used by companies, universities, and research institutes around the world across areas such as gaming, XR, assistive tech, research, and automotive.
  
Headquartered in Stockholm, Sweden, Tobii has offices across Europe, North America, and Asia, and is listed on Nasdaq Stockholm (TOBII).
  
We are now looking for a software engineer to join our team in Stockholm. This is an exciting and broad role where you will build stable and scalable cloud platforms for our engineering teams and products.
  
  

The Opportunity
  
As a Platform Engineer at Tobii, you will develop and operate the internal platforms and infrastructure that support our engineering organization. You will help create scalable, reliable, and easy-to-use development environments that enable teams to move faster with less operational complexity.
  
A key part of the role is supporting large-scale compute and workloads. We operate infrastructure that handles hundreds of concurrent builds and distributed workloads, where scalability, reliability, and cost efficiency are critical.
  
We think about internal platforms as products, with a strong focus on developer experience, usability, adoption, and continuous improvement.
  
  

Your mission
  
* Build and improve internal platforms, automation, and developer workflows across cloud environments
* Explore and implement AI-assisted engineering workflows and platform capabilities
* Improve scalability, reliability, and cost efficiency in distributed compute environments
* Collaborate closely with product engineering teams and operations to solve platform and infrastructure challenges


We hope you have
  
* Solid experience as a software engineer or platform engineer
* Experience working with cloud platforms such as Azure, AWS, or GCP
* Curiosity around AI-driven development workflows and modern engineering practices
* Interest in platform engineering, infrastructure, automation, and developer tooling
* Experience with Python/C++ or similar programming languages
* Experience with technologies such as Kubernetes, Docker, Terraform, GitHub Actions, or similar tooling


Who are you?
  
We hope you enjoy collaborating closely with others, sharing ideas, and contributing to a culture where people help each other grow. You are curious by nature and motivated by solving problems in a thoughtful and practical way. You like taking ownership and thrive in environments where continuous improvement is part of everyday work.
  
  

Location
  
This role is based in Stockholm, Sweden, with a 60/40 hybrid work policy.
  
  

The recruitment process
  
Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. Please submit your application as soon as possible. We look forward to hearing from you!

title: Backend / Platform Engineer
description:
Own the business logic and infrastructure that moves dental radiographs from capture to AI analysis and back into the clinical record — reliably, securely, and at scale. You will be the primary architect of the platform, logic and API layers that every other part of our product depends on, including integrations with partners.
  
What you’ll do
  
Design and maintain the ingestion pipeline that connects Dentail AI to imaging hardware and practice management systems across the world.
  
Build and operate the cloud infrastructure (Google Cloud) that runs our services to ensure high availability, scalability and smooth engineering operations.
  
Drive reliability and observability across the platform — SLAs, alerting, incident response, and post-mortem culture.
  
Work directly with ML engineers to optimise the path from model output to structured clinical data.
  
  

What we’re looking for
  
MSc, or similar, in computer science or a related field.
  
4+ years of backend engineering experience, with at least 2 years owning a production platform or data pipeline at scale.
  
Strong proficiency in Python and/or Go; comfortable across the Google Cloud ecosystem
  
Clear written communicator; our team is often async and documentation matters.
  
**Bonus:** A track record of building and operating systems in regulated environments — financial services, healthcare, or similar.

title: Senior Microsoft 365 & Azure Cloud konsult
description:
**Senior Microsoft 365 & Azure Cloud Consultant – skapa säkrare och smartare molnlösningar**


Vi söker en senior Microsoft Cloud-konsult som vill göra verklig skillnad hos våra kunder. Hos oss får du en nyckelroll i att guida organisationer mot moderna, säkra och hållbara IT-miljöer – där teknik möter verksamhetsnytta.


På IT-Total arbetar vi nära våra kunder och deras verksamhet. Vi kombinerar teknisk spets med affärsförståelse och långsiktiga relationer. Nu söker vi dig som vill vara en trygg rådgivare, ta ansvar för komplexa kundmiljöer och hjälpa organisationer att lyckas med sina molnresor.

**Om rollen**  

Som Senior Microsoft 365 & Azure Cloud konsult hos oss har du en central och verksamhetsnära roll. Du fungerar som rådgivare, specialist och drivande kraft i kundernas transformation mot moderna molntjänster.


Du arbetar nära både verksamhet och IT och ansvarar för att omsätta affärsbehov till säkra, skalbara och hållbara lösningar. Rollen kombinerar strategisk rådgivning med praktiskt genomförande och passar dig som trivs nära både teknik och kund.


I rollen kommer du bland annat att:

* Leda och driva projekt inom Microsoft 365 och Azure
* Designa lösningar med fokus på säkerhet, skalbarhet och kostnadskontroll
* Vara rådgivare inom cloud adoption, governance och informationssäkerhet
* Hålla workshops och utbildningar som stärker kundens egen förmåga
* Implementera och vidareutveckla lösningar inom Microsofts ekosystem
* Bygga långsiktiga kundrelationer och bidra till kundens fortsatta utveckling

**Din bakgrund**  

För att lyckas i rollen tror vi att du har flera års erfarenhet av att arbeta med Microsoft 365 och/eller Azure samt en god förståelse för hur moderna molnlösningar skapar affärsvärde. Du har erfarenhet av lösningsdesign och implementation i komplexa miljöer och känner dig trygg i frågor som rör identitet, säkerhet och Entra ID.


Du är van vid att arbeta nära kund och har erfarenhet av att driva dialoger på både teknisk och verksamhetsmässig nivå. Att facilitera workshops, skapa struktur i projekt och guida kunder genom förändringsresor är naturliga delar av ditt arbetssätt. Du kommunicerar obehindrat på både svenska och engelska.


Det är extra meriterande om du har erfarenhet av Microsoft Purview, känner till Cloud Adoption Framework och Well-Architected Framework eller har arbetat med säkerhetsfrågor inom reglerade eller samhällsviktiga verksamheter.

**Vem är du?**  

Som person är du trygg, engagerad och lösningsorienterad. Du bygger förtroende genom tydlig kommunikation och trivs i rollen som rådgivare där du får kombinera tekniskt djup med affärsförståelse. Du tar ansvar, driver arbetet framåt och motiveras av att skapa verkligt värde för kunden. Samtidigt har du ett genuint intresse för säkerhet, molnteknik och moderna arbetssätt.

**Varför kommer du att trivas på IT-Total?**  

IT-Total föddes ur idén att vara det bästa företaget för medarbetare inom sin bransch, därför välkomnar vi dig till en arbetsplats som ger dig glädje, stimulans och möjlighet att växa inom det område du brinner för. Vi är en innovativ arbetsgivare som satsar på din framtid och jobbar i teknikens framkant med ett högt säkerhetstänk. Med säkerhetsprövade medarbetare och säkerhetsklassade lokaler- och datahallar garanterar vi trygghet och integritet för våra kunder.  

  

**Vi erbjuder:**

* En himmelsk fredagsfrukost med kollegorna


Hybrid arbetsplats


Roligt på jobbet - allt från träning till resor och spelkvällar


Ett välfyllt lager av frukt, nötter, gudomligt kaffe från en Kunglig Hovleverantör om så önskas.


Fredagsfika tillsammans


Ett stenkast till både shopping på Mall of Scandinavia och natursköna Brunnsviken som är perfekt för löpning och lunchpromenader


✔ Goda och trygga anställningsvillkor.


Frihet att styra din egen utveckling och ditt eget arbete, vi tror på "Work life balance"


‍ ‍ Kollegor i världsklass som bryr sig


Goda parkeringsmöjligheter


️‍ ️ Generöst friskvårdsbidrag

**Varmt välkommen med din ansökan i formuläret nedan.**  

  

**Har du några frågor gällande tjänsten?**  

Tveka då inte att höra av dig till ansvarig rekryterare:  

Kajsa Jordansson, Talent Acquisition Manager, IT-Total  

Mail: kajsa.jordansson@it-total.se
  

Tel: 070 -729 19 82  

  



Avdelning
Konsult
Platser
Solna
Distansarbete
Hybridarbete
Kontakt Kajsa Jordansson Talent Acquisition Manager

title: Automotive Software Platform Verification Senior Engineer to Accenture Industry X
description:
ccenture is hiring an **Automotive Software Platform Verification Senior Engineer** to strengthen our automotive team in Gothenburg.


In this cross-functional role you will be **on-site technical representative** at customer location to provide direct support and contribute to developing and maintaining **developer platform infrastructure** and **software-in-the-loop (SiL) environments.** The role focuses on faster, more reliable, and more automated verification of embedded software. This role requires frequent presence at client premises in Gothenburg, thus we look for candidates having existing working permit/rights in Sweden or right to work in EU.


Industry X is Accenture’s capability focused on engineering and manufacturing transformation — it’s about how products are designed, built, connected, and operated across their entire lifecycle.

 **What You’ll Do**

* For the Developer Platform, design and maintain a unified, hermetic developer container (Docker/OCI) for toolchains, build systems, SDKs and test runners (Linux, QNX, FreeRTOS, Android)
* Build and manage CI/CD pipelines; maintain container registry with versioned images and changelogs
* Establish a clear E2E developer journey: bring-up scripts, debugging workflows, log/trace collection
* Enable and optimize workloads on cloud infrastructure (AWS, Terraform)
* Enable developers and testers to work with embedded automotive software stacks in pure-software environments (software-in-the-loop, SiL)
* Implement multi-VM / container communication on protocol layers (Ethernet, SomeIP, CAN)
* Develop helper tools and integrate simulation models into the SiL environment
* As on-site customer interface, serve as on-site technical liaison; support integration, verification and vehicle testing at customer facilities
* Facilitate rapid issue identification, debugging and resolution; communicate feedback to development teams
* Provide hands-on support for software flashing, configuration and milestone reviews

  

**Primary skills for the role:**

* More than 5 years of experience, with experience in customer-facing roles and automotive projects
* Python and Bash proficiency
* Cloud / IaC: Terraform, AWS (or equivalent), developer environments at scale
* CI/CD: GitHub Actions, pipeline-as-code, artifact management
* Containers: Docker/OCI multi-stage builds, registry management, hermetic environments
* Automotive protocols: Automotive Ethernet, SomeIP, CAN, LIN, UDS — including in software-only setups
* Simulation tooling, especially Vector SIL-Kit
* Strong SOC/MCU software integration knowledge
* Good communication skills; fluent English

**Nice if you have also some of these skills:**

* OSP Cuttlefish / Android virtual device experience
* Build systems: Bazel (preferred) CMake/Ninja, Yocto/BitBake, AOSP, QNX SDP, ARM cross-compilation
* Yocto SDK and sysroot
* SBOM generation and supply-chain tooling in CI
* MISRA/AUTOSAR static analysis tools (Polyspace, Axivion, Coverity)
* Distributed build systems (Bazel, IncrediBuild)
* Virtual targets: QEMU/KVM, GDB/KGDB remote debugging
* C++ experience

**What we can offer you**

* Competitive compensation and benefits
* Flexible work hours with a good work-life balance
* Significant development opportunities and support for progressing in your professional career.
* Rich opportunities to develop your business acumen, consulting toolbox, industry knowledge, technical and professional skills.
* Meaningful and exciting client projects
* A phenomenal opportunity through working with a great variation of projects and industries.
* A dedicated mentor following your career journey and a strong learning culture.
* Responsibility, trust, and an inclusive work environment
* The opportunity to get involved in Accenture´s volunteering, Women in Tech, sport- and social groups.
* Access to industry and capability specialists in all corners of the world with outstanding international knowledge and experience
* A good and inclusive working environment, where you will work with inspiring and skilled colleagues and managers.

***Ready to join us?****Apply now by sending your CV and relevant certificates.*

title: Platform Engineer inom Molntjänster i Sundsvall
description:
Placeringsort
Sundsvall
Diarienummer
FK 2026/014733  



*Varje år fattar Försäkringskassan 21 miljoner beslut som påverkar människors liv och hela samhället. Vi betalar ut cirka 260 miljarder kronor varje år. Det är allas skattepengar vi har hand om. Det är ett stort ansvar. Därför tänker vi nytt och använder den senaste tekniken för att förenkla och förbättra, utveckla och leverera it-tjänsterna för den svenska socialförsäkringen. Välkommen till en av Sveriges största IT-avdelningar.*Inom produktområdet Plattform & Integration ansvarar vi för hela livscykelhanteringen av våra produkter inom Integration, Plattform, Container, Databas och IAM. Detta inkluderar utveckling, implementering, drift och förvaltning för att säkerställa stabila och effektiva plattformstjänster.



Kunskapsutbyte är en integrerad del av vårt arbete. Vi erbjuder kontinuerlig kompetensutveckling, möjligheter att utforska ny teknik och tid för lärande, vilket skapar en dynamisk miljö för professionell utveckling.


**Webbplattform, Containerteknik och AI**
-----------------------------------------



Som plattformsingenjör inom produktområdet Plattform och Integration blir du en viktig del i utvecklingen och förvaltningen av Försäkringskassans plattformstjänster. Du kommer att arbeta i en privat molnmiljö där du hanterar hela livscykeln för plattformens olika komponenter, från utveckling och implementering till drift och optimering.



I rollen får du utforska och implementera ny teknik, inklusive AI och open source-lösningar, samt ta ansvar för webplattformar byggda på containerteknik. Du kommer att vara involverad i vidareutveckling av självservicegränssnitt, samt förbättring och optimering av plattformsinfrastrukturtjänster.



Arbetet innebär även tvärfunktionellt samarbete med andra tekniska team, där du får möjlighet att dela kunskap, bidra till automatiseringsprocesser och vidareutveckla Försäkringskassans system.



Vi söker dig som trivs med problemlösning, innovation och effektivisering och som har en stark teknisk grund inom containerteknik, infrastrukturtjänster och programmering.



Tjänsten innebär att du hanterar sekretessbelagda uppgifter vilket ställer höga krav på rätt hantering. Därför är det viktigt att du som ska arbeta hos oss har högt säkerhetsmedvetande.



Det är ett jobb för dig som vill vara med och ta ansvar.



Vi söker dig som


* har högskoleexamen eller erfarenheter och kvalifikationer som kan prövas likvärdiga
* har erfarenhet av Containerteknik som Kubernetes eller Docker
* har erfarenhet av arbete i Linux
* agerar på eget initiativ och tar ansvar för att nå goda resultat
* samarbetar effektivt med andra för att nå gemensamma mål
* har ett systematiskt och noggrant arbetssätt
* kan förstå och analysera komplexa frågor
* har god kommunikativ förmåga
* har goda kunskaper i svenska, i tal och skrift.


Det är meriterande om du


* har erfarenhet av att arbeta med Försäkringskassans interna system och plattformar
* har tekniska färdigheter inom data beskrivningsformat, till exempel Yaml, Json och Xml
* har tekniska färdigheter inom Infrastrukture as Code IaC
* har tekniska färdigheter inom Datasäkerhet
* har tekniska färdigheter inom IAM.
* har tekniska färdigheter i Python, Bash eller Golang
* har kunskap om Git och GitOps
* kunskap om Cloud Native-produkter
* kunskaper om Helm
* kunskaper om AI.


Vi lägger stor vikt vid dina personliga egenskaper.


**Övrigt**
----------



En tjänst, tillsvidare, heltid, med tillträde enligt överenskommelse. Provanställning kan bli aktuellt. I enlighet med Försäkringskassans riktlinje för säkerhet genomförs bakgrundskontroller i denna rekrytering. Vissa anställningar hos oss innebär deltagande i säkerhetskänslig verksamhet.



För att bli anställd i en sådan tjänst krävs godkänd säkerhetsprövning enligt säkerhetsskyddslagen (2018:585). Möjlighet till distansarbete finns om arbetet tillåter. Placeringsort: Sundsvall.



Vill du veta mer om Försäkringskassan som arbetsgivare? Besök fk.se/jobbahososs


**Kontaktpersoner**
-------------------



Chef: Johan Åberg, 010-114 61 52 (för frågor om tjänsten). HR-specialist: Beatrice Sporre, beatrice.sporre@forsakringskassan.se, 010- 114 47 79 (för frågor om rekryteringsprocessen). Fackliga representanter är för ST Marie Nordenfjäll, Saco-S Ann Wiström, Seko Eliza Grundström. Dessa nås via vår växel 08-786 90 00.


**Ansökan**
-----------



Välkommen med din ansökan senast den 21 juni 2026. När du ansöker bifogar du ditt CV. Vi använder inte personligt brev i våra rekryteringar, du får istället besvara ett antal urvalsfrågor som är relevanta för tjänsten. Ladda upp dina examensbevis, betyg och intyg i samband med din ansökan. Urval sker löpande under rekryteringsprocessen.



Har du skyddade personuppgifter eller ingen tillgång till bank-id ringer du den HR-specialist som finns namngiven i annonsen så får du hjälp med att skicka in din ansökan.


Vi ska i allt vi gör, agera med utgångspunkt i principen om alla människors lika värde, rättssäkerhet och med god service.

title: Data & Analytics Intern
description:
**About Us**
------------


Legora is redefining how legal work gets done. Not built for lawyers, built with them. We work alongside the world’s best legal teams, who expect excellence, precision, and speed, and we hold ourselves to the same bar.  

  

Our AI-native workspace lets legal professionals move faster, think more clearly, and operate with sharper precision. By analysing thousands of documents in minutes and powering end-to-end workflows, we cut through complexity, teams can focus on what matters: judgment, strategy, and outcomes.  

  

1,000+ customers across 50+ countries trust us, including Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Linklaters, White & Case, Dentons, and Barclays. We’ve scaled to **$100M+ in ARR**, with teams across Europe, North America and APAC, and continue to expand through acquisitions including Qura, Walter AI and Graceview.  

  

We partner with world-class performers: including Aaron Judge and the New York Yankees, Ludvig Åberg (and his caddie), and campaigns featuring Jude Law.  

  

Joining Legora means three things.

* We **lean in:** ownership over titles, outcomes over intentions.
* We **fight for excellence:** high standards, direct, ego-free feedback.
* We **grow together:** as a team and with our customers.


Mission before ego. Everyone contributes. No one coasts.


If you’re driven by impact, pace, and raising the bar. This is the place.


As a **Data & Analytics Intern** at Legora, you'll spend the summer building something real: an internal analytics agent that the rest of the company can actually use. Think of this as a role that sits squarely between software engineering and data science — you'll be designing and deploying an agentic system on top of our data stack, not prototyping something that lives in a notebook.  

  

The agent you build should be able to answer questions about our business, navigate our data models, and get better the more it's used. You'll own the project end-to-end: scoping, building, deploying, iterating with real users on the data team and beyond. You'll be supported by senior data and engineering folks, but the direction and the build are yours.


This is a hands-on, ship-it summer. The bar is that what you build is still in use after you leave.  

  

**What you will be doing:**

* Design, build, and deploy an internal analytics agent that helps Legora employees explore and understand our data.
* Architect the agent so it improves over time — retaining context across conversations, learning from prior interactions, and getting smarter the more it's used.
* Integrate the agent with our data warehouse (Snowflake), semantic layer, and dbt models so it can answer questions grounded in real, modelled data.
* Build the surrounding infrastructure: evaluation harnesses, observability, guardrails, and a deployment pipeline that lets the agent evolve safely.
* Partner with data, product, and engineering to shape what the agent should do — and what it shouldn't.
* Ship iteratively, gather feedback from real internal users, and improve the system on a short loop.

**Who you are**

* Currently studying or recently completed a degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, or a related technical field.
* You've built and deployed agentic systems before — not just called an LLM API in a script, but designed agents with tools, state, and real production constraints in mind.
* Comfortable across the stack: you can write production Python, you understand how to model data (dbt, SQL, warehouse design), and you've worked with modern LLM frameworks and orchestration tools.
* You think like an engineer about reliability, evaluation, and iteration — not just like a prototyper.
* Familiarity with at least some of: dbt, Snowflake (or similar cloud warehouses), semantic layers, vector stores, LLM observability tooling, and modern agent frameworks.
* A bias toward shipping. You'd rather get something crappy in front of users in week two than have something perfect in week ten.
* But most importantly, you are someone who
* "Gets stuff done" and understands building a $10bn company isn't always glamorous and takes hard work and long hours
* Strives in a fast-paced environment where the answers aren't always clear and processes are few

 ***Practicals & Logistics***

**Location:** Stockholm HQ, on-site  

**Duration:** Summer 2026 (exact dates flexible)  

**Team:** Data & Analytics, sitting close to Engineering & Product

 **Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**


At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Staff Software Test Engineer - Vehicle Access Systems
description:
**Location**
------------


Sweden | Lund


**Employment Type**
-------------------


Full time


**Department**
--------------


Technology and Innovation


  
**About Us**
============


Rivian and Volkswagen Group Technologies is a joint venture between two industry leaders with a clear vision for automotive’s next chapter. From operating systems to zonal controllers to cloud and connectivity solutions, we’re addressing the challenges of electric vehicles through technology that will set the standards for software-defined vehicles around the world.


The road to the future is uncharted. By combining our expertise across connectivity, AI, security and more, we’ll map a new way forward. Working together, we’ll create a future that’s more connected, more intelligent, more sustainable for everyone.


**Role Summary**


We are looking for a Staff Software Engineer in Test to lead the automation strategy and execution for our Vehicle Access Systems (VAS) – the features that enable Digital Key (CCC), NFC, BLE, UWB, key fobs, and related access experiences across current and future vehicle platforms.

  

This is the first hands-on, technical, and team-building role for the VAS System Test in the Lund office, serving as the foundational Software Engineer in Test for the site. You will work closely with the development team to define the overall test strategy, execute testing, and report results to both development and test teams. You will own all test activities, including building and maintaining the test environment and ensuring product quality within VAS. Your remit will be to eventually design, build, and grow a high-performance automation/test team in Lund, while providing day-to-day technical direction, executing the strategy, and acting as the primary bridge between Lund and our teams in North America and other regions.

  

You will own end‑to‑end test and automation for VAS - from framework design to CI/CD integration, test benches, and on‑vehicle validation - while coordinating closely with site leaders, partner teams on the Lund site, and global stakeholders to ensure alignment on priorities, scope, and quality.


**Responsibilities**


* **Lead the Lund VAS automation & system test team**


	+ Provide technical and people leadership for the local VAS test/automation team in Lund, including mentoring engineers, setting goals, and supporting career growth together with local management.
	+ Coordinate daily priorities and test activities with local development, product, and site leadership, ensuring Lund’s efforts are tightly aligned with global VAS goals.
	+ Act as the primary interface between Lund and North America VAS teams, helping to close gaps in communication, test coverage, and release readiness across time zones.
* **Own automation strategy for VAS**


	+ Define and drive the test automation strategy for Vehicle Access features (Digital Key, NFC, BLE, UWB, key fobs, immobilizer, sleep/wake, user profiles) across multiple vehicle platforms and programs.
	+ Balance unit, integration, system, and end‑to‑end automation to maximize coverage and early defect detection (shift‑left testing).
* **Design and build automation frameworks**


	+ Architect, implement, and maintain Python‑based automation frameworks and tooling for VAS benches and vehicles (e.g., Pytest‑based suites, internal tools, log parsers and dashboards).
	+ Integrate automation into CI/CD pipelines (GitLab/Jenkins or similar) and ensure tests are reliable, repeatable, and visible to engineering and leadership.
* **Scale system and end‑to‑end testing**


	+ Develop automated scenarios that span mobile, cloud, and vehicle for key flows such as enrollment, pairing, lock/unlock, passive entry/exit, immobilizer, and error handling.
	+ Leverage test benches, HIL setups, and real vehicles to validate behavior under realistic and adverse conditions (signal quality, power, network variation, hardware faults).
* **Lead quality for feature and release cycles**


	+ Partner with VAS System Test, development, product, and program teams to define test plans, coverage goals, and exit criteria for features and releases.
	+ Use data from automated and manual runs to drive clear release recommendations and continuous improvement of test suites (flakiness reduction, gap closure, tech‑debt burn‑down).
* **Mentor and collaborate across locations**


	+ Mentor other automation, system test, and integration engineers in Lund and remote sites, sharing best practices in test design, coding standards, and debugging of complex, distributed systems.
	+ Work closely with cross‑functional partners (firmware, mobile, cloud, security, HIL, tools) in Lund, North America, and other locations to align on testability, logging, diagnostics, and lab infrastructure needs.
* **Invest in tooling and process**


	+ Help evolve our VAS test strategy, metrics, dashboards, and reporting so that quality is transparent and actionable across the organization.
	+ Contribute to continuous improvement of how we plan, track, and execute work (e.g., MR testing, regression strategy, bench management, and “test as a service” models).
	
	
	**Qualifications**
	
	
	**Education & experience**
	
	
		- Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Electrical/Computer Engineering, Software Engineering, or a related field.
		- 8+ years of hands-on experience in software test automation or quality engineering, including system‑level testing of complex, distributed or embedded products.
		- Prior experience in automotive, embedded systems, connectivity, or other safety‑critical domains is strongly preferred.
	+ **Technical skills**
	
	
		- Strong programming skills in Python/Java/C/C++ for test automation and tooling (Preferred Pytest or similar), including test frameworks, libraries, and utilities.
		- Experience integrating automated tests into CI/CD (GitLab, Jenkins or similar), including pipelines for benches and vehicles.
		- Solid understanding of hardware‑software integration and test environments (HIL, benches, vehicle bring‑up, diagnostic tools such as Ride/UDS/CAN).
		- Familiarity with BLE/UWB/NFC, CAN/Ethernet, and security/identity concepts for access systems is a plus.
	+ **Collaboration & leadership**
	
	
		- Proven experience leading test or automation teams, including setting direction, prioritizing work, and coaching engineers across locations.
		- Ability to coordinate with multiple leaders and stakeholders on the Lund site (development, product, program, systems, lab) and keep everyone aligned on status, risks, and next steps.
		- Comfortable working with teams across time zones and cultures, building strong relationships with partners in North America and other regions, and keeping communication clear and proactive.
		- Excellent communication skills (verbal and written), able to simplify complex technical topics and drive decisions with data and clear arguments.You are reliable, accountable, and willing to go the extra mile when needed—owning issues end‑to‑end, following through on commitments, and continuously pushing the team and process to the next level.


**Bonus Points**


Experience with:
* Test data and results visualization (dashboards, metrics for build health and release readiness).
* AI‑assisted test authoring or prioritization, or large‑scale regression management.
* Working in globally distributed teams and collaborating across multiple sites and partners.
* Have embedded development experience
* Use/build AI tools in testing.

**Total Rewards**
-----------------


Full-time positions include base salary, eligibility for an annual performance bonus, and eligibility for equity.


In addition to base salary, Rivian and Volkswagen Group Technologies offers benefits tailored to the local market. For more information on the benefits available for full-time employees, check out our Global Benefits Site.


External candidates can apply for this role through the Rivian and Volkswagen Group Technologies careers site (https://rivianvw.tech/#careers).


**Equal Opportunity**
---------------------


Rivian and Volkswagen Group Technologies is committed to creating a diverse environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender expression, gender identity, genetic information or characteristics, physical or mental disability, marital/domestic partner status, age, military/veteran status, medical condition, or any other characteristic protected by law. We are also committed to ensuring compliance with all applicable fair employment practice laws regarding citizenship and immigration status.


Rivian and Volkswagen Group Technologies is committed to ensuring that our hiring process is accessible for persons with disabilities. If you have a disability or limitation, such as those covered by the Americans with Disabilities Act, that requires accommodations to assist you in the search and application process, please email us at candidateaccommodations@rivian.com.


**Candidate Data Privacy**
--------------------------


Rivian and Volkswagen Group Technologies” may collect, use and disclose your personal information or personal data (within the meaning of the applicable data protection laws) when you apply for employment and/or participate in our recruitment processes (“Candidate Personal Data”). This data includes contact, demographic, communications, educational, professional, employment, social media/website, network/device, recruiting system usage/interaction, security and preference information. Rivian and Volkswagen Group Technologies may use your Candidate Personal Data for the purposes of (i) tracking interactions with our recruiting system; (ii) carrying out, analyzing and improving our application and recruitment process, including assessing you and your application and conducting employment, background and reference checks; (iii) establishing an employment relationship or entering into an employment contract with you; (iv) complying with our legal, regulatory and corporate governance obligations; (v) record keeping; (vi) ensuring network and information security and preventing fraud; and (vii) as otherwise required or permitted by applicable law.


Rivian and Volkswagen Group Technologies may share your Candidate Personal Data with (i) internal personnel who have a need to know such information in order to perform their duties, including individuals on our People Team, Finance, Legal, and the team(s) with the position(s) for which you are applying; (ii) Rivian and Volkswagen Group Technologies affiliates; and (iii) Rivian and Volkswagen Group Technologies’ service providers, including providers of background checks, staffing services, and cloud services.


Rivian and Volkswagen Group Technologies may transfer or store internationally your Candidate Personal Data, including to or in the United States, Canada, and the European Union and in the cloud, and this data may be subject to the laws and accessible to the courts, law enforcement and national security authorities of such jurisdictions.


If you provide a mobile telephone number as part of your application or during the recruitment process, Rivian and Volkswagen Group Technologies may use that number to contact you via SMS text message for recruitment-related purposes, including scheduling, logistics, and status updates. Message and data rates may apply. You may opt out of SMS communications at any time by replying STOP to any text message you receive from us. Consent to receive SMS messages is not a condition of applying for or being considered for employment.
