title: Nordic Looker Practice Lead
description:
Are you ready to shape the future of Business Intelligence across the Nordics?
   

  

 At
 **Devoteam** 
 , we deliver innovative technology consulting, and as a premier
 **Google Cloud Partner** 
 we sit at the forefront of the data space. We are looking for a
 **Nordic Looker Practice Lead** 
 to own our Looker capability, drive regional growth, and cement our position as the go-to partner for Google Cloud data solutions.
   

  

 Reporting directly to our
 **Head of Data** 
 , you will own the Looker strategy across the Nordic region. It is a hybrid role that combines technical mastery, strategic leadership, and alliance management.
   

  

 Curious about our projects? We got you: Check out our success stories
   

  

**Job Description**
**What you will do**
* Grow the practice: Lead and scale our Looker engagements across the Nordics, spot new business opportunities, and advise clients on modern analytics.
* Own delivery quality: Act as the guardian of technical excellence — set the standard for LookML development, review the team's work, and ensure our deliverables exceed client expectations.
* Strengthen the Google alliance: Build deep, trust-based relationships with Google's local and regional teams, positioning Devoteam as the preferred partner for selling, architecting, and delivering on Looker.
* Mentor and lead: Develop and upskill our data team, fostering a culture of continuous learning around the modern data stack.
* Partner with sales: Connect market trends with new Looker features and capabilities — including conversational analytics — to shape what we take to clients.


 Want to know more? Check out our people stories on Youtube
   

  

  

  

**Qualifications**
**Who you are**
 You are a seasoned data professional who lives and breathes data modelling. You understand that Looker isn't just a visualisation tool — it's a semantic layer that transforms how a business works with data.
   

  

* Looker expertise: Several years of hands-on experience designing, developing, and deploying enterprise Looker solutions. You know LookML deeply, and caching policies, PDTs, and hub-and-spoke architectures are second nature.
* The modern data stack: Strong proficiency on Google Cloud, specifically BigQuery — query optimisation and partitioning come naturally.
* Solution architecture: You design BI and analytics solutions end to end — from the warehouse and modelling layer, through the semantic layer, to consumption. You make deliberate trade-offs about where logic lives (dbt vs LookML and PDTs), how it's governed, and how it scales across teams and environments.
* Data modelling and engineering: Deep SQL knowledge and hands-on experience with transformation tools such as dbt.
* Leadership and advisory: A track record of leading technical teams and the confidence to command the room in stakeholder presentations.


**The ultimate bonus 🚀 Conversational analytics:** 
 Experience bridging data insights and natural language puts you at the very top of our list.
   

  

**Additional Information**
 Devoteam is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace where everyone feels valued and respected. We encourage applications from individuals with diverse backgrounds and experiences.
   

  

* Competitive Compensation: Enjoy a competitive compensation package with an uncapped commission structure, rewarding your hard work and success.
* Comprehensive Benefits: Benefit from comprehensive health insurance, a retirement plan, and generous paid time off.
* Growth Opportunities: Take advantage of excellent opportunities for professional development and career advancement within our growing Google Cloud practice.
* Cutting-Edge Technology: Work with the latest Google Cloud technologies and contribute to impactful projects that transform businesses.
* Dynamic Culture: Be part of a dynamic, supportive, and collaborative team culture that values innovation and personal growth.
* Flexible Work Arrangements: Enjoy flexible work arrangements that allow you to balance your professional and personal life.


 We do plenty of activities, like meet-ups, hackathons, and partnerships. Don’t lose any of them: follow us on LinkedIn and our Instagram page
   

  

**Ready to lead the change?**
 If you have the expertise to scale our Looker practice and the drive to partner with Google, we want to hear from you. Apply today.
   

  

 Devoteam is an equal-opportunity employer. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds and are committed to building an inclusive team.

title: Senior Business Operations Specialist
description:
IFS is a billion-dollar revenue company with 7000+ employees on all continents. We deliver award winning enterprise software solutions through the use of embedded digital innovation and a single cloud-based platform to help businesses be their best when it really matters–at the Moment of Service™.
   

  

 At IFS, we're flexible, we're innovative, and we're focused not only on how we can engage with our customers but on how we can make a real change and have a worldwide impact. We help solve some of society's greatest challenges, fostering a better future through our agility, collaboration, and trust.
   

  

 We celebrate diversity and accept that there are so many different perspectives in this world. As a truly international company serving people from around the globe, we realize that our success is tantamount to the respect we have for those different points of view.
   

  

 By joining our team, you will have the opportunity to be part of a global, diverse environment; you will be joining a winning team with a commitment to sustainability; and a company where we get things done so that you can make a positive impact on the world.
   

  

 We're looking for innovative and original thinkers to work in an environment where you can #MakeYourMoment so that we can help others make theirs.
   

  

 If you want to change the status quo, we'll help you make your moment. Join Team Purple. Join IFS.
   

  

**Job Description**
 This role will report to the Business Operations Global Head and will work with the Regional Management team to provide Business Operations function expertise including but not limited to:
   

  

* Support regional management on preparing data in advance for weekly forecast; Engage in the forecast call to present pipeline data, taking notes of the action items and driving execution.
* Ensure accuracy and transparency of CRM Data, enabling correct business decision making
* Support the Region on quarterly business reviews and other management presentations as requested
* Be a point of contact for sales team with regards to system access, CRM, reporting tools, and data.
* Onboarding and ongoing support for Sales Reps on the actual execution of the sales processes and ensuring compliance in each step.
* Play a key role in sales support and operations, regularly engaging with sales reps and diverse functions in the organization. Provide guidance in terms of process, pricing, and contracts
* Work with the regional team to analyze pipeline data and movement
* Follow up on deal actions and related updates with Sales rep. Ensuring the tools are properly updated by the respective sales rep and all opportunities are correctly reflected in CRM and the forecasting dashboard.
* Coordinate with other departments during the sales cycle and service delivery for the post-sales activities.
* Coordinate with the License Generation Team regarding the customer order. Attend to all their inquiries and provide the necessary clarifications
* Ensure CRM is updated with a complete set of documents i.e., Signed contracts, Pricing, Purchase Orders, Proof of delivery and other related documents.
* Ensure alignment with the global team on the product releases, sales process, and tools that need to be rolled out in the Region.
* Look for opportunities to improve standard operating processes for sales operations and be part of the project to improve these processes.


**Qualifications**
 A Business Operations would be expected to have a strong skills in the following areas:
   

  

* Comprehension of the end-to-end sales process of the business
* Worked with and managed CRM in previous roles
* Strong understanding of AI tools and the ability to leverage them effectively in day-to-day operations
* Excellent excel skills
* Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal, in English and French language - other languages is a plus.
* Good financial acumen


**Additional Information**
 Your background will be:
   

  

* A similar role in a large software or technology organization in the given geography.
* Appreciation of and ability to work within a matrix environment
* Have previously designed, built and improved business processes
* Professional experience in Finance and/or Auditing

title: Lead Platform Engineer
description:
**Job Description**

 We’re looking for a Lead Platform Engineer
 
 to take ownership of our Kubernetes-based multi-cluster internal developer platform that powers mission-critical microservices across the organization.
   

  





 This role is equal parts technical leadership, hands-on engineering, product thinking
 **,** 
 and communication. You will shape the direction of the platform as a product, obsess over developer experience, and ensure the platform is intuitive, reliable, and well-documented.
   

  





 You will work closely with product teams, architects, and engineering managers to evolve our platform into a highly automated, self-service ecosystem that accelerates developer productivity and enables secure, seamless deployments at scale.
   

  





 Our technical stack includes Azure Kubernetes Service, Envoy Gateway, Opentelemetry Operator, LGTM Stack, Crossplane, KEDA, Cert Manager, External DNS, Kyverno, External Secrets Operator, Argo CD, Helm, Bicep, Github Actions.
   

  

  

  




**Qualifications**

**WHAT YOU'LL DO**



* Own the technical direction of our Kubernetes platform with a strong focus on developer experience and operational excellence.
* Treat the platform as a product. This involves gathering feedback, identifying friction, and continuously improving workflows and interfaces.
* Design, build, and operate cloud-native infrastructure with an everything-as-code mindset.
* Write production-quality code (Bash, YAML, Bicep, Github Actions) - this is a hands-on engineering role.
* Drive complex features and refactoring efforts across provisioning, deployments, observability, and cluster lifecycle management.
* Partner closely with product teams to understand real workflows and deliver a frictionless self-service experience.
* Mentor engineers through collaboration and example, fostering a culture of curiosity, ownership, and learning.
* Raise the bar on maintainability and engineering craftsmanship through strong attention to detail.



  

  





**WHO YOU ARE:**



* 5+ experience in platform engineering, SRE, or application engineering roles.
* Deep expertise in Kubernetes, containers, and the cloud-native ecosystem.
* A builder mindset - you write and ship code regularly.
* Excellent technical writing skills.
* Experience operating in public cloud environments (Azure, AWS, or GCP) with modern IaC and GitOps tooling.
* Proven track record improving developer workflows, self-service capabilities, or internal platforms.
* Understanding of networking (TLS, DNS, HTTP, GRPC, proxies).
* Experience with modern observability practices and tooling (instrumentation, metrics, logs, tracing, profiling).
* Pragmatic, collaborative, and humble approach to technical leadership.
* High attention to detail and a bias for architectural simplicity and clarity.



  

  





**WHY JOIN US**



* **Make a real impact** 
 - you’ll have significant influence over the direction of the platform and shape how platform engineering works across the company.
* **Platform at the center of transformation** 
 - as the organization continues its transition to microservices, the platform sits at pole position, with rapid growth and growing strategic importance.
* **Modern, cloud-native stack** 
 - we invest in staying current with the cloud-native ecosystem and evolving our architecture accordingly.
* **Ideas matter here** 
 - we value curiosity, experimentation, and thoughtful innovation, and give engineers space to turn ideas into reality.
* **Supportive, low-ego culture** 
 - you’ll work with collaborative teammates who care about helping each other succeed and building great things together.


**Additional Information**

***Please note***
 : As our culture and teamwork are very important to us, four days (80%) per week of in-office presence in our office are required.
   

  





 This is a full-time position with placement in
 **Stockholm** 
 .
   

  





  

  





**WHO WE ARE:**
  

 Shaping the future of fashion with people, data, and tech. The fashion and retail industries are going through a transformation, driven by customers' technology and sustainability expectations. At H&M Group, we want to shape the future of fashion and lifestyle by harnessing the power of smart tech and data. With our 74-year history of innovation, we understand the need to collaborate and co-create with engineers and tech specialists around the world to achieve our vision.
   

  





  

  





**WHAT WE OFFER!** 
  

  

 You are joining a unique value-driven culture, a large tech network and community where you can be yourself. Besides the obvious perks such as staff discount card, flexible work life, learning communities, wellness benefits, parental benefits etc. There are endless opportunities to experiment and grow in any direction that you want, and when you grow, we grow. Being a major player gives us countless opportunities to make a real impact and shape the future.

title: Endpoint Management Engineer
description:
**Company Description**
 At Sobi, each person brings their unique talents to work as a team and make a difference. We are dedicated to developing and delivering innovative therapies to improve the lives of people who live with a rare disease. Our edge comes from our team of people and our commitment to patients.
   

  

 Here At Sobi, Our Mission And Culture Get Us Excited To Come To Work Every Day, But Here Are a Few More Reasons To Join Our Team
   

  

* Competitive compensation for your work
* Emphasis on work/life balance
* Collaborative and team-oriented environment
* Opportunities for professional growth
* Diversity and Inclusion
* Making a positive impact to help ultra-rare disease patients who are in need of life saving treatments


**Job Description**
 The IT department, a part of IS/IT, are responsible for architecture, strategy, maintenance and support of SOBIs Microsoft platforms, infrastructure such as clients, servers, network. The IT department consist of five teams – Infrastructure, Service desk, IT security, Digital Workplace & AI and Automation & Integration. IT work according to Sobi’s quality framework based on ITIL.
   

  

 As a Digital Workplace IT Operations Manager for Device and Applications you will work in our Microsoft cloud environment. Managing devices and applications in our Windows client and iOS environment, to ensure security, compliance, and productivity. You will enroll and configure devices in Intune, deploy and update applications, monitor health and performance and troubleshoot issues across Windows and iOS.
   

  

**Main Responsibilities**
* Ownership of key elements in the Digital Workplace team’s responsibility
* Lead and coordinate Service requests, Changes and roadmap activities within your area
* Manage and coordinate Digital Workplace incidents in ServiceNow
* Suggest and deliver continuous improvement regarding the individuals appointed systems/products/solutions with a focus on identifying opportunities for process improvement
* Support in managing and coordinating training of ServiceDesk and other departments of Sobi regarding the individuals appointed systems/products/solutions
* Serve as SME for the Digital Workplace & AI, including, but not limited to device management, Intune, RoboPack, applications and trainings
* Application and OS Lifecycle management (Intunes/RoboPack/Windows Autopatch), Apple device/Phone OS and Application Lifecycle Management (Intune/Apple Business Manager)
* Knowledge sharing inside the Team to build a stronger resilience, to protect against interruptions in our delivery


**Qualifications**
* Relevant education within IT or equivalent practical experience
* Previous experience of working as a part of a device team in a Microsoft cloud environment (Intune), ability to create and package applications and configurations in Intune, good knowledge in trouble shooting Windows OS, Windows application and iOS application, good knowledge in Microsoft Autopilot and Autopatch, basic to intermediate PowerShell scripting.
* Previous experience of application management in Robopack, or similar solution
* Ideally, intermediate knowledge about security and compliance related to device management in a global company setting.
* Team player, flexible and adoptable. Pro-active and solution oriented.
* Fluent in Swedish and English

title: Senior Solutions Architect
description:
(FEQ427R299)
   

  

**About The Role**
 At Databricks, we are looking for a Senior Solutions Architect to join our Nordics Hunter team. You will be the technical lead for our most strategic new engagements in the region, with the mission to establish Databricks as the main data & AI platform on Greenfield accounts. You aren't just a technical resource; you are a strategic advisor who raises the bar for how our customers build and reason through data solutions.
   

  

 You will thrive in rapid growth environments and high-paced customer engagements, creating structure in ambiguous situations and driving commitment from senior stakeholders even when priorities shift. Whether you come from a pre-sales background or have deep technical expertise in complex systems, your ability to manage high-level stakeholders and deliver measurable business outcomes is what will make you successful.
   

  

**The Impact You Will Have**
* Technical Strategy & Leadership: Lead the technical strategy for Greenfield accounts within the Nordics - assessing a customer’s architecture and provide strategic guidance and deep technical expertise addressing problems that span multiple systems, teams, and stakeholders, from data & AI practitioners, business domains and all the way up to C-level.
* Executive Advisory: Act as a trusted advisor to VP+ and BU-level leaders, driving alignment during high-tension negotiations and ensuring long-term technical commitment.
* Raise the Bar: Abstract learnings from your builds into reusable patterns and guidance for the wider Databricks community, anticipating objections before they surface.
* Drive Adoption: Move beyond "proof of concept" to deliver outcomes the business can measure, using adoption signals to ensure sustained customer impact.
* Mentorship: Coach junior and professional SAs on technical discovery and complex use-case prioritization, helping the team maintain high standards at sustained velocity.


**What We Look For**
* Note - you do not need to fulfil every single requirement to be a strong candidate. If you are excited about this role and have related experience, we encourage you to apply.\*
* 8+ years of experience in technical and client-facing roles.
* Deep expertise across the data landscape—including Data Engineering, Data Warehousing, and GenAI—with a strong "AI-Builder" mindset.
* Proven ability to influence senior executives and drive alignment across competing constraints and evolving goals.
* Recognized as an experienced architect who can confidently lead technical discussions and solve high-complexity, multi-account architectural challenges.
* Ability to execute through ambiguity: A track record of identifying emerging risks early and mobilizing others to act, even when full information is not available.
* Pre-sales experience is a plus, but we value deep technical expertise and stakeholder management skills above all as key must-haves.
* Language & Location: Must be based in Sweden or Denmark with excellent communication skills suited for top-tier customer interactions.


**About Databricks**
 Databricks is the data and AI company. More than 10,000 organizations worldwide — including Comcast, Condé Nast, Grammarly, and over 50% of the Fortune 500 — rely on the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform to unify and democratize data, analytics and AI. Databricks is headquartered in San Francisco, with offices around the globe and was founded by the original creators of Lakehouse, Apache Spark™, Delta Lake and MLflow. To learn more, follow Databricks on Twitter, LinkedIn and Facebook.
   

  

**Benefits**
 At Databricks, we strive to provide comprehensive benefits and perks that meet the needs of all of our employees. For specific details on the benefits offered in your region click here.
   

  

**Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion**
 At Databricks, we are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive culture where everyone can excel. We take great care to ensure that our hiring practices are inclusive and meet equal employment opportunity standards. Individuals looking for employment at Databricks are considered without regard to age, color, disability, ethnicity, family or marital status, gender identity or expression, language, national origin, physical and mental ability, political affiliation, race, religion, sexual orientation, socio-economic status, veteran status, and other protected characteristics.
   

  

**Compliance**
**If access to export-controlled technology or source code is required for performance of job duties, it is within Employer's discretion whether to apply for a U.S. government license for such positions, and Employer may decline to proceed with an applicant on this basis alone.**

title: Data Engineer
description:
Northmill Bank is a challenger bank at the intersection of technology and finance, committed to revolutionizing the way people manage and protect their financial well-being. We are creating a different kind of banking experience,
 **digital yet personal** 
 .
   

  

 Northmill Bank was founded in 2006 and have grown to over 240 employees in 3 countries, 4 000 merchants and 600 000 end users. We use the latest technology to develop safe, smart, and user-friendly products for our customers. They are the sole reason why we do what we do. We are a 100% cloud-based product company where technology is the driver to create smarter banking products.
   

  

 Grab this opportunity to be a part of us and our journey!
   

  

**About Our Tech Stack**
* Snowflake
* DBT
* Dagster
* AWS
* Fivetran
* ThoughtSpot
* Cursor with OpenAI codex


**About The Role**
 As a Data Engineer at Northmill Bank you will be part of the data lifecycle, focus on transforming/modeling the data to fit final business requirements and serving data for BI/reporting. You will be a part of the cross-functional Analytics team consisting of Data Engineers, Data Scientists and Analysts who serve the whole bank in terms of data for insights, predictive modeling, reporting and all other potential use cases.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Utilize DBT to model and transform data, ensuring it's clean, structured, and ready for analysis, reporting and other operational systems.
* Continuously monitor and optimize data flows to proactively drive us forward.
* Support data needs in relation to specific analytical tasks.
* Collaborate with product and product engineering teams to acquire and build data models for new business and products data modelling.
* Collaborate with the Platform team on building and maintaining ingestion pipeline, guarantee high quality data into Snowflake.
* Stay up-to-date with the latest industry trends and emerging technologies in data engineering.


**Qualifications**
* 3+ years of experience in a data engineering role
* Proficiency in SQL/Snowflake, DBT and Python.
* Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
* Experience with analytical data modelling.
* Experience with cloud platforms, especially AWS, is a plus.
* Experience with using Agentic AI in IDE is a plus.
* Have an interest in the underlying business and its data needs.


**What We Offer**
* A fantastic office in a prime Stockholm location with great spaces and views
* An independent role with the opportunity to make a real impact
* Great opportunities for professional development
* Health - 5 000 kr health care allowance
* Conference abroad every other year
* Breakfast and fruits every day, as well as "holy fika” each Friday
* Regular after work and celebrated successes at the office


**Apply today and be a part of Northmill!**
 Northmill Bank is a tech company with a banking license aiming to improve financial life, offer merchants smart solutions and erase the borders between in-store and online.
   

  

 Northmill creates a more personal and relevant experience for people and companies, helping more than 2,500 merchants and 250,000 end users.
   

  

**Trustpilot** 
 4.5 of 5
   

  

**Cloud** 
 100 %
   

  

**End users** 
 250 000
